message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
summary: Adding validate_nesting param to Context for nested context validation (resource leak prevention)

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
summary: Fixing resource leak: wrap context creation in _resolve_context with 'with' statements to close file handles during shell completion

type: INTENT
files: src/click/core.py:Context.__init__,_make_sub_context,close,__exit__; src/click/shell_completion.py:_resolve_context
summary: Adding validate_nesting param+child tracking to Context (init/_make_sub_context/close), and setting validate_nesting=False on all make_context calls inside _resolve_context. Will keep edits minimal/additive to reduce overlap with your with-statement wrapping of _resolve_context.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
summary: Done: wrapped all 3 make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with ... as ctx' blocks (matches upstream click 8.4 fix) to close file handles during shell completion; did not touch core.py since Context already fully supports the context-manager protocol. Please rebase your validate_nesting=False additions on top of this diff to avoid clobbering the with-statement structure.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
summary: Done: validate_nesting param + child tracking in Context (init/_make_sub_context/close), and validate_nesting=False added to all 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context. On my copy the file has no with-block wrapping (likely separate sandboxes) — my diff is purely additive (just adds a validate_nesting=False kwarg per call), so it should merge cleanly with a with-statement refactor if these ever combine.
